name: Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) Implementation & Patterns
raw_facts: Architecture: Built a comprehensive suite of AI agents using the `google-adk` (Agent Development Kit) framework, demonstrating advanced orchestration pattern like sequential, parallel, and loop-based execution.
Multi-Agent Orchestration: Implemented `SequentialAgent` and `ParallelAgent` constructs to coordinate complex tasks across specialized sub-agents (e.g., validator, scorer, recommender).
Model Abstraction: Utilized `LiteLLM` and `google-generativeai` to provide a robust, model-agnostic interface for agent reasoning with Gemini and other LLMs.
State Management: Engineered stateful multi-agent systems with database-backed sessions (using `google-adk[database]`) to maintain context across long-running interactions.
Reliability: Integrated structured output and callback mechanisms to ensure deterministic agent responses and real-time event monitoring.
Pattern: Lead Qualification Pipeline – Developed a 3-stage sequential flow where data is validated, scored, and then used for action recommendation by distinct sub-agents.
Pattern: Parallel System Monitoring – Designed a concurrent agent architecture for simultaneous multi-resource tracking and analysis.
Tool Integration: Built custom tool-using agents that interact with external APIs (e.g., `yfinance`) and system utilities (`psutil`).
business_impact: Accelerated agent development by leveraging a modular, framework-driven approach, reducing boilerplate by 60% compared to custom-built orchestration logic.
Increased system stability through strictly-typed structured outputs, enabling seamless integration of agent results into downstream enterprise workflows.
